title: cleome augustinensis
intro: Cleome augustinensis, also known as the Spider Flower, is a perennial flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and aid in the treatment of digestive problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Cleome augustinensis can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
